conversation.message: How have you been feeling lately?
Irritable. I get annoyed at the smallest things. Like if someone is talking loudly on the phone or the traffic is slow. It’s just little things, but it gets on my nerves.
Has that irritability made it harder to wind down or get a good night's sleep?

Yeah, definitely. I lie there at night and my mind just won't stop. I think about everything I need to do tomorrow, or what someone said at work, and I can't switch off. I just lie there going over it all in my head.
When your mind is racing like that at night, does it make it harder to get excited about things the next day?
Yeah, it does. I wake up and I'm already tired. I used to love my job, but now it just feels like a chore. I don't have the energy to care anymore.
You mentioned your job feels like a chore now—has that lack of energy and interest spilled over into things you used to do for fun?
Yeah. I used to love reading, but I haven't picked up a book in months. I just don't have the patience for it. I used to spend hours doing it, but now I can't even sit still for five minutes.
When you think about the future, does it feel like things will get better, or does it seem kind of hopeless?
It feels hopeless. I don't know how to get this back. I've tried taking days off, trying to relax, but it doesn't work. I just feel like I've lost it.
You mentioned that things feel pointless - when did that start feeling this strong?
I don't know, maybe a few months ago? It just crept up on me. I used to be the one making plans, but now I just let life happen to me.
You mentioned you just let life happen to you now—does that feeling of being on autopilot make you feel restless or on edge during the day?
Yeah, it does. I feel like I'm just going through the motions. I look at people around me, and they're all doing things, living their lives, and I'm just... I'm just stuck.
When you feel stuck like that, does it ever make it hard to focus on tasks or make decisions?
Yeah, it does. I'll be at work and I'll have to choose between two things, and I'll just freeze. I can't decide. It’s like I'm not in control anymore.
You mentioned feeling like you're not in control anymore—what does that feel like on a day-to-day basis?
It feels like I'm just a passenger. I'm just sitting there, watching life happen, and I can't do anything to stop it.
When you say nothing matters, what parts of life feel most empty lately?
Everything. Work, relationships, everything. It all feels hollow. I don't know what the point is anymore.
